summary: Mixed‑quality Shoreline show with soaring SOTM and strong New Speedway
blurb: The set opens with a bright Alabama > Greatest Story and builds to a powerful New Speedway Boogie. Highlights include an emotionally charged Standing On The Moon, the monks‑chant drum/Space segment, and a solid encore of Lucy In The Sky With Diamonds. Recording quality varies, but the soundboard capture is clear despite uneven playing.
review: June 2, 1995 at Shoreline Amphitheatre captures the Dead in a state of flux – moments of brilliance punctuated by occasional lapses. Jerry’s guitar tone is richer than many ’95 outings and he soars on Standing On The Moon and New Speedway Boogie, yet his vocals sound nasal and mumbled on Bird Song and Saint Of Circumstance. Bob Weir steps up, stitching songs together when Garcia wanes and delivering confident vocals on the monks‑chant segment. Vince’s keyboards sparkle during SOTM but slip out of key in the more languid passages. The rhythm section is a study in contrast: Mickey and Bill earn kudos for an “all‑time best” drum chant that flows into Space, but several reviewers note timing hiccups that disturb the flow. The first set kicks off with Alabama > Greatest Story, then meanders through slower jams before lifting in the second set. Standout moments – New Speedway Boogie, the monks chant, and a soaring SOTM – make this show worth hearing, even though uneven sections keep the overall experience mixed.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings label the show as mixed while one is outright positive. Consistent praise for Standing On The Moon, New Speedway Boogie, monks‑chant/Space and SOTM provides modest positive sentiment, but repeated criticism of Bird Song, Saint Of Circumstance and overall uneven flow tempers enthusiasm.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with clear, well‑balanced mix that captures the highlights despite occasional performance unevenness.

key_highlights: Soaring performances of Standing On The Moon and New Speedway Boogie.
Energetic monks‑chant/drums > Space segment that stands out as a highlight.
Strong opening with Alabama > Greatest Story sets an upbeat tone.
Inconsistent playing on Bird Song and Saint Of Circumstance creates uneven flow.
song_highlights: Lucy In The Sky With Diamonds

band_performance.Jerry: Fuller guitar tone and powerful leads on SOTM and New Speedway Boogie, but vocals sound nasal, mumbled, or strained on Bird Song and Saint Of Circumstance.
band_performance.Bob: Keeps songs together when Garcia wanes, delivers solid vocal parts especially during the monks‑chant segment.
band_performance.Brent: Vince’s keyboards shine on SOTM with melodic leads, yet he slips out of key and his vocal tone is criticized on slower numbers like Bird Song.
band_performance.Mickey: Drumming praised for an all‑time best chant leading into Space, though timing issues appear elsewhere in the set.
band_performance.Billy: Shares drum duties; some reviewers note cohesion problems during jams, but overall contributes to the strong rhythmic drive of the monks‑chant and Space.

must_listen_sequences: Lucy In The Sky With Diamonds
